summary: First testing run for ZenStay. Backend: 15/16 pytest cases pass (94%). Frontend: all major flows verified (landing, 6 listing cards, dB badges, FR/EN language toggle, dark mode, listing detail with login-gated booking, /admin guard redirect). One backend failure: GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} returns 500 due to a third-party library (emergentintegrations) pydantic validation bug on Stripe metadata. Checkout creation itself works and persists payment_transactions.

critical.issue: Returns 500. emergentintegrations CheckoutStatusResponse validation fails: 'metadata Input should be a valid dictionary [type=dict_type, input_value=<StripeObject ...>]'. Stripe SDK returns StripeObject for metadata; the Pydantic model in emergentintegrations expects dict. Server.py code path is correct; failure is inside library at checkout.py:204. Webhook-driven status updates still work (reservation.statut moves to paid on webhook event), but client-polled /payments/status endpoint is broken until lib fix or a workaround is added.

action_items: Fix GET /api/payments/status/{session_id}: either (a) catch CheckoutError and fall back to retrieving stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve directly via the stripe SDK and coerce metadata to dict, or (b) cast metadata to dict before constructing CheckoutStatusResponse inside a thin wrapper, or (c) report/upgrade the emergentintegrations library. The success-page polling on the frontend will currently fail until fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_session sets cookie with samesite=none + secure=True which is correct for cross-site preview, but the cookie is sent via XHR only when frontend uses credentials: 'include' — confirm AuthContext uses withCredentials/credentials include for /api/auth/me (Bearer fallback is supported so OK either way).
Reservation montant uses _nights which forces minimum 1 night even if dates are inverted; consider returning 400 for date_depart <= date_arrivee.
MAKE webhook posts hote_email; ensure that is intentional (it is, for host notification — correct per spec).
server.py is 678 lines — still manageable but approaching the 700-line limit recommended for split into routers/auth/payments modules.
No rate-limiting or idempotency on POST /api/reservations or /payments/checkout (out of scope for MVP).
test_credentials: admin@zenstay.com (role=admin, seeded). Backend testing uses manually inserted user_sessions tokens (test_admin_session_zs, test_user_session_zs) via Authorization: Bearer header, cleaned up after run. Real auth E2E not automatable (Emergent Google OAuth).
seed_data_creation: Seed listings (6) and seed users (admin/alice/ben) are created at backend startup; tests rely on them being present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite in /app/backend/tests/test_zenstay.py covers auth (cookie+Bearer), public listings privacy (hote_email removed), reservation auth+computation, admin CRUD/toggle/list, Stripe checkout creation and 404 on unknown session. Only the /payments/status/{session_id} test fails — confirm fix by re-running pytest after main agent patches the Stripe status handler.
rca of the issue: RCA: emergentintegrations 0.x (checkout.py:204) constructs CheckoutStatusResponse(Pydantic BaseModel) from Stripe SDK Session object. Stripe SDK returns `metadata` as a `StripeObject` (dict-like but not a true dict subclass for Pydantic v2 strict typing). Pydantic v2 dict_type validator rejects it. Reproduction: POST /api/payments/checkout (works), then GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} -> 500. Mitigation in app code: wrap stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status in try/except CheckoutError, on failure use stripe SDK directly: `s = stripe.checkout.Session.retrieve(session_id)`; build response with `metadata=dict(s.metadata or {})` and payment_status/status from `s`.
